Root meaning 'strong' became a noun meaning 'one instance of strength' by adding ة at the end
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this strength belongs to أُو۟لُوا۟ (the possessors/people of)
This is a special word that means 'possessors of' or 'people of'. It's used to show that someone owns or has something. In this ayah, it's describing نَحْنُ (we) to say 'we are people of strength'.
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to what came before it.

steps.m: to be mighty, strong, or severe

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to make the basic noun form. This word shows what the أُو۟لُوا۟ (possessors) have.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is indefinite (not specific) and is being described by the word شَدِيدٍ (mighty/severe) that comes after it.
Root letters ش-د-د got vowels (َ ِ) to make a word meaning 'strong'
Added ي to show this is a strong, lasting quality
Added tanwin kasra to show this word describes بَأْسٍ (might) in the phrase 'people of might'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the action itself or the matter being dealt with. This word is connected to إِلَيْكِ (to you) in the ayah.
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show this is the subject being discussed
Added و to connect this to the previous statement about strength and might, showing this is an additional point in their response
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي with sukun before adding the pronoun
Added the feminine 'you' pronoun كِ at the end to show who the action is directed towards
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding hamza at start and changing ending - makes it a command
Added ى to make it a feminine command since it's addressing the queen
Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result or consequence
This is the basic question word used to ask about things
Added ذَا (meaning 'this') to مَا to make the question word stronger. This combination is asking about what command the queen will give.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'you' as subject
Changed to address a female person giving the command
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Adding تْ at the end changes 'he' to 'she'. This word is the subject (doer) of إِنَّ (indeed) in this ayah.

This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more emphasized. The doubled ن (shown by ّ) makes it mean 'indeed' instead of just 'if' or 'that'. Here it emphasizes what the queen is saying about kings.
Added vowels to create the singular noun 'king' from the root meaning
Changes from 'king' to 'kings' by changing the vowels to u-u-oo pattern
Added 'the' and accusative case ending because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)

steps.m: when (introduces the condition that دَخَلُوا۟ follows)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces a condition. It connects two events - in this case, it shows what happens when kings enter a town. The word دَخَلُوا۟ (they entered) comes right after it to complete the condition.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: they entered (subject of أَفْسَدُوهَا - they corrupted it)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people (they) did the action. The ُ before وا is needed for correct pronunciation. This word is connected to أَفْسَدُوهَا (they corrupted it) - it's telling us what the same group of people did.
Root letters connected with a fat-ha (a) and sukoon (stop mark) to make 'gathering'
Added ة to make it a specific instance of gathering (i.e., a settlement)

steps.m: a village (being entered by الْمُلُوكَ - the kings)
steps.n: Added tanween (double line) at the end to show this is the object - the thing being entered
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes it mean 'to cause' the action
Adding وا at the end shows multiple people did the action
Adding هَا shows what they corrupted (it/her)
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action

steps.m: and they made/set/placed (referring to what kings do to a town's people)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this to the previous statement about what kings do. Also added the elongation mark (ـٓ) and small و (۟) which help with proper recitation but don't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show strength
Creates an intensive adjective meaning 'one who possesses might/power'
Forms the broken plural indicating a small group (3-10) of mighty ones
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of جَعَلُوا (they made)
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form meaning 'people' or 'inhabitants'
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection to something else

steps.m: its people (referring to قَرْيَةً, the town)
steps.n: Added هَآ at the end to show it belongs to the town (قَرْيَةً) mentioned earlier in the verse
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from a verb (doing something) to describing someone by adding ي - like changing 'humbled' to 'humble'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple people with this quality
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object of the verb جَعَلُوا (they made/rendered)
This is a demonstrative word meaning 'that'
Adding ك at the beginning changes it to mean 'like that'
Adding و connects this to the previous statement. This phrase describes how 'they do/act' (يَفْعَلُونَ) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past to present by adding يَ at start and ُ at end - this is a common pattern for verbs

steps.m: they do/make/act (referring to the kings mentioned earlier in the verse)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people (kings) are doing the action. This connects back to الْمُلُوكَ (the kings) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a word that adds emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'certainly'
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Added ى at the end to show 'I' am doing the action. The نَّ changes to نِّ because ى comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix with sukun and kasra vowels shows who does the sending (like changing 'send' to 'sender' in English)
Adding ة at the end makes it feminine, like changing 'sender' to 'female sender'
Adding the tanween (ٌ) shows this is talking about any sender, not a specific one
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change

steps.m: to them (referring to the messengers mentioned in ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ)
steps.n: Added هِم which means 'them' (masculine plural). This refers back to the messengers (ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ) mentioned later in the verse.
Root letters h-d-y got short vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make them readable as 'hadee'
Added shadda (double letter) on ي and added ة to turn 'guiding' into 'a gift' - like turning 'guide' into 'present'
Added بِ at beginning to show 'with'

steps.m: with a gift (that she is sending)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show it's indefinite and follows the preposition بِ. This gift is being sent by the Queen of Sheba to Solomon.
Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show who does the action. This word connects to مُرْسِلَةٌ (sending) in the verse.
Added ة to make the word feminine since it refers to a female subject (the Queen of Sheba)

steps.m: and so (I am) a female one who watches/waits
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous action of sending a gift

steps.m: and so (I am) a female watcher/waiter
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah to make the word indefinite nominative
This is an interrogative particle meaning 'what'

steps.m: with what (asking about the means/way something will be done)
steps.n: The ا at the end of مَا is dropped when بِ is added at the beginning. This word is asking about how/with what means the messengers will return (يَرْجِعُ ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action pattern
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive participle form to show who receives the action
Added ون to make it plural for multiple people
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and َ for grammatical case
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after something else
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
This is a proper noun (name) borrowed from Hebrew that doesn't have an Arabic root. It refers to Prophet Solomon, son of Prophet David.
The name gets a fatha (َ) at the end because Solomon is being addressed or spoken about in this sentence. The alif is written as a special long alif (ٰ) which is common in the Quran.
Added vowel marks to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make 'he extended'
Changed to present tense with prefix تُ and vowel changes
Added plural marker for multiple people doing the action
Added question marker at beginning
Added نِ to show the action is being done to 'me'
Added alif after second root letter to form the noun for wealth/money

steps.m: with wealth/money (related to أَتُمِدُّونَنِ - 'will you provide me')
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with' and tanwin kasra at end to show this is answering 'with what?' in relation to the question about providing
Basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Changed alif to alif madd (آ) - this is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: then what (connects to سُلَيْمٰنَ قَالَ - Solomon said)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what happened before. In this ayah, it connects to when Solomon spoke.
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long vowel
Added نِ at the end to show the action is being done to 'me'
Added a fatha mark at the end to show proper pronunciation
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added damma to show this is the subject
Basic noun pattern showing the quality of goodness
Adding double damma makes it indefinite and shows it's the subject
Starting with the two separate words that combine to form this word: مِن (from) and مَا (what)
The ن from مِن combines with the م from ما to make a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark)
The first م also gets stronger (shown by another ّ mark) to match the sound that came before
The final ا is made longer (آ). In this ayah, it means 'than what' because it's comparing what Allah gave Sulaiman to what they gave him.
Changed to Form IV pattern which makes the meaning causative. The final ي becomes ى because it's at the end.
Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel
Added كُم at the end to show the recipients (you all) of the giving

steps.m: rather/but rather (contrasts with previous statement about giving wealth)
steps.n: This is a particle that shows contrast. Here it's used to transition from Sulaiman rejecting their wealth to explaining why - because they are the ones who rejoice in gifts, not him.

steps.m: you all (plural masculine, subject of تَفْرَحُونَ)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'you all' (for a group of males or mixed group). In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb تَفْرَحُونَ (you all rejoice).

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb 'he guided' by adding vowel marks
Added marks to make it mean 'a gift' instead of 'guided' - like how a gift guides someone to happiness
Changed ending to show possession
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with'
Added كُمْ at end to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
Added ت at beginning to show present tense and 'you' as the doer

steps.m: you all feel joy/happiness (referring to the people receiving the gift in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show that many people are doing the action. This refers to the people who were happy about giving a gift to Prophet Sulaiman.
Added sukun on first letter, kasra on middle letter, and sukun at end to make basic command form
Added hamzat al-wasl (ٱ) at beginning to help pronunciation of command form
This is a basic direction word that shows movement or connection towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attachment
Added هِمْ at the end to show this means 'to them'. This word connects to ارْجِعْ (return) to say 'return to them'
Connected the root letters together to form the base word

steps.m: we come (connected to بِجُنُودٍ - with armies)
steps.n: ن at start shows 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next part of the verse where it mentions coming 'with armies'
Adding ل at the beginning shows this will definitely happen in the future

steps.m: so surely we will come (after ٱرْجِعْ - return)
steps.n: Adding ف shows this happens as a result of the previous command 'return'
The نّ adds strong emphasis showing this is a serious threat or promise

steps.m: so we will definitely come to them
steps.n: هم shows who we're coming to - a group of males

steps.m: to gather or assemble (like troops or armies)

steps.n: Changed from root meaning 'gather' to plural noun 'armies' by adding 'u' sounds (like ju-nood)

steps.m: with armies (connected to فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّهُم - we will come to them)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show these armies are being used as tools or means. This connects to the earlier word فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّهُم (we will come to them), showing HOW they will come - with armies!

Added the tanween (double kasra) at the end to show these are indefinite armies - meaning they're special or impressive armies that the listeners don't know about yet. The next part of the ayah confirms this by saying they won't be able to resist these armies!
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates قِبَلَ (power/capability)
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger, emphasizing there is absolutely no power/capability
The root meaning 'to face' becomes a word meaning 'before/in front of' by adding specific vowels. Like saying something is facing the armies.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added هُ to show it refers to a single male or thing

steps.m: to/for them (referring to the army/soldiers mentioned in جُنُودٍ)
steps.n: Added م to change from singular to plural, referring to the army/soldiers mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic preposition showing how something is done or used
Added kasra to help with pronunciation when connecting to next word
Added feminine pronoun ها to show what the preposition connects to. Here it refers back to the armies (جُنُود) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Basic meaning of leaving or exiting a place
Added نُ prefix to show 'we' are doing the action
Added لَ to show purpose - 'in order to/so that'
Added emphatic نّ suffix to add strong emphasis/certainty to the action

steps.m: and we will definitely make (others) exit
steps.n: Added conjunction prefix to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and we will definitely make them exit
steps.n: Added object pronoun suffix هُم (them) to specify who will be made to exit (referring to the people being addressed in the previous ayah)
This is a basic preposition showing movement away from something or someone
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word connects to the previous word in pronunciation
Added ها at the end to mean 'from it' - ها refers back to something mentioned before (like pointing back to something)
The alif is written with a small vertical line (آ) instead of regular ا. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Basic meaning of being humbled or lowered in status
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into an adjective describing someone who is humble or lowly
Changed the word to show it's talking about multiple humbled people
Added tanween to show this describes their state or condition
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they' and sukun (ْ) shows where to stop

steps.m: and they (who are صٰغِرُونَ - in a state of being humbled)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word connects to صٰغِرُونَ (humbled ones) to show their state.
Basic meaning of being small or insignificant
Shows someone who is in a state of being small or lowly
Makes the word refer to many people. This describes how 'they' (هُمْ) are feeling.
Added vowel marks to make it past tense. This shows someone (he) did this action in the past.
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ because of the weak letter و - this is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle
Root letters أ-ي-ي combine to make أَيّ, a word used to ask 'which' or 'who'
The word gets a damma mark (u sound) on top to show it's the main focus of the sentence
Adding ها changes this from a question word to a way of addressing someone
Adding vowel marks makes it a past tense verb
The hamza changes to waaw due to the damma coming after it
Added ال to make it specific - refers to the specific council being addressed by Solomon

steps.m: the noble assembly, the council (as main subject)
steps.n: Added the dot (damma) to show this group is doing the action

steps.m: O noble assembly! (calling out to them)
steps.n: Added alif at end to show we're calling out to this group
Adding the vowel mark makes it readable
Adding damma to show this word is the subject
Adds كُمْ to specify that the question is directed to multiple people being addressed
Added يَ at the beginning to show who is doing the action (he) and that it's happening now or in the future
Added نِ and ي at the end to show the action is happening to 'me'. The نِ is added to protect the ي from changing

steps.m: he comes/arrives to me (in jussive mood)
steps.n: Added sukoon at the end to show this is in jussive mood (used after certain words like لم)

steps.m: to construct or establish something elevated (like a throne, roof, or trellis)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic noun meaning 'throne'. This is the object being brought in the story.

steps.m: her throne (referring to Queen of Sheba's throne)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show it belongs to her (the Queen of Sheba). The ِ (kasra) before هَا connects the word to هَا.
Added بِ at start to mean 'with'. This word is part of the question 'which of you can bring me her throne?' (أَيُّكُمْ يَأْتِينِى بِعَرْشِهَا)
Changes the root letters 'to face/come before' into a common word meaning 'before' by adding the vowels a and silent b
Added fatha at the end because this word is describing when something happens in relation to يَأْتُونِى (they come to me)

steps.m: that (connects to يَأْتُونِى - they come to me)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the main idea 'before' to what happens 'they come to me', similar to saying 'before that they come to me' in English.
Added يَ at the start to show 'he' is doing it in present time, and added vowel marks to make it readable
Added و at the end to change from 'he comes' to 'they come'
Changed the ending to ونِى to show 'they' are doing the action to 'me'

steps.m: to submit, surrender, or be at peace

steps.n: Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show someone who does the action of submitting. Like changing 'submit' to 'submitter'.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people who submit, not just one person
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense. This word shows what عِفْرِيتٌ (a powerful jinn) did.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle

steps.m: to be strong, powerful, or cunning

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and form a noun meaning someone/something with this quality
Adding ت at the end makes it refer to a specific type of powerful being

steps.m: a powerful and cunning being (subject of قَالَ - said)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb قَالَ (said) in this ayah
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'. The sukun shows where the word stops.
The letter ن merges with the letter after it, shown by the shadda mark (ّ), but keeps the same meaning
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word الْجِنِّ (the jinn), showing it means 'from the jinn'

steps.m: to hide or be hidden from sight

steps.n: Adding the kasra changes it from a verb (to hide) into a noun (hidden beings)
The kasra at the end shows this is possessed by something
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of). This word describes what kind of عِفْرِيتٌ (powerful being) is speaking.
This is a pronoun without a root - it starts with the basic form أن which indicates first person
Adding ا at the end makes it specifically mean 'I' (first person singular)

steps.m: I (subject of ءَاتِيكَ - I will bring)
steps.n: The small circle (۠) is added as a pronunciation mark to show the long vowel sound. This word is the subject of the verb ءَاتِيكَ (I will bring) in this ayah.
Changed to first person present tense verb meaning 'I bring'

steps.m: I bring to you (responding to سليمان's request)
steps.n: Added كَ to show who receives the action. This is part of a jinn's response to Prophet Sulaiman's request.
Added kasra (ِ) to help pronunciation
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'with it' (referring to the throne mentioned earlier in the story)
Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide
Forms a noun meaning 'before' from the root letters
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing when something happens in relation to 'you stand' (أَن تَقُومَ) in the ayah
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it connects the main verb ءَاتِيكَ (I will bring it) with تَقُومَ (you rise), showing when the action will happen.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. The و is a weak letter that stays from the root.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added fatha at end to show this is part of a longer phrase
Basic preposition showing the starting point or origin. Here it connects 'before' with 'your place/position', showing when the jinn will bring the throne.
Added مَ at the start to make it mean 'place of' and changed the root letters to make مَقَام meaning 'place of standing'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by something
The doubled م adds emphasis to show this is a well-established or firm position
Added كَ at the end to show it belongs to 'you'
This is a word that makes statements stronger and more certain
Added ي at the end to show this is about me/I
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
Basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when pronouns are attached

steps.m: upon him/it (referring to the throne mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show 'him/it' - refers back to what was mentioned before

steps.m: to be/become strong, to have strength

steps.n: Adding shadda creates an adjective showing a permanent quality

steps.m: indeed/truly strong (describing عِفْرِيتٌ - a powerful jinn)
steps.n: The prefix لَ adds emphasis ('indeed/truly'), while the tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing the powerful jinn mentioned earlier
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who has this quality strongly. Also added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation.
Added tanween damma to show this describes the subject (عِفْرِيتٌ - an ifrit) in the sentence. The tanween shows it's indefinite, like saying 'a trustworthy one'.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed middle weak letter و to ا, which is common in hollow verbs
This is the base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things. The ى at the end is just a spelling variation of ي.
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it specific. This word introduces the person who had knowledge from the Book.
The root letters got vowel marks (kasra and fatha) to make it a preposition meaning 'with/at/by'
Added pronoun suffix هُۥ meaning 'him' to show who has/possesses something. In this ayah, it refers back to the person who had knowledge from the Book.
Changes the action of knowing into the concept of knowledge itself
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject of the phrase 'he had knowledge'
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. It shows where something comes from or starts.
The shadda (ّ) doubles the م sound, which happens when certain letters come before مِن
The fatha (َ) at the end connects this word to the next word الْكِتٰبِ (the book), showing that something is from the book
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from the action of writing to the thing that contains writing
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after مِنَ (from/of) which requires the possessed form

steps.m: I (subject of ءَاتِيكَ - I will bring it to you)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'I'. In this ayah, it's used by the person who has knowledge from the Book, saying 'I will bring it to you' (referring to the throne of the Queen of Sheba).
Changed to present tense first person (I)
Added ك to show the action is done to 'you'
Basic preposition that shows how something is done
Added هِ to show what/who the preposition affects. Here it refers to the throne mentioned earlier in the story.
Added ۦ which is just a pronunciation mark that doesn't change the meaning
Basic root meaning showing the concept of facing or coming before something
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'before' that can be used as a preposition
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'before your glance returns' (أَن يَرْتَدَّ)
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects to يَرْتَدَّ (returns/comes back) to show what happens before something else.
Connected the root letters and added the shadda to show doubled letter
Added reflexive pattern to show the action happens to oneself

steps.m: he returns back (before طَرْفُكَ - your glance)
steps.n: Changed to present tense to show action happening now. This word describes what happens before 'your glance' (طَرْفُكَ) returns.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'towards' or 'to'. It doesn't come from a root.
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added كَ at the end to show this means 'towards you'. In this ayah, it's used to describe the direction of bringing something to the person being spoken to.
The ُ at the end shows this is the subject or doer of the action
Adding كَ at the end makes it possessive - 'your glance'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَرْتَدَّ (returns/blinks).
This is a particle that shows something happened right after something else, like saying 'as soon as' or 'when' in English

steps.m: Then when (connects to رَءَاهُ - he saw it)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after the previous action. In this ayah, it connects 'when' to 'he saw it'
The weak letter ي at the end changes to ا, and the hamza أ changes to ء because it comes after a fatha and before an alif
Added هُ to show who was seen (him). This هُ refers back to something mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to settle, become fixed, or remain in place
steps.n: The root letters represent the concept of settling or becoming fixed in place
Doubling the middle letter adds emphasis to show something is firmly settled
Adding مست shows something is seeking or requesting to be settled

steps.m: being settled (object of رَءَاهُ/he saw it)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb رَءَاهُ (he saw it)
This is a common preposition in Arabic that comes from the idea of being present with something
Adding dots and marks to the basic letters to make the word mean 'at/with/near'
Adding the fatha mark at the end makes the word ready to use in a sentence
Adding هُ at the end shows this means 'with him'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word عِلْمٌ (knowledge), so together it means 'he had knowledge'
This form turns the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of speaking'
Added vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb about 'he'
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Basic pointing word that shows something is near
Added ذا to make it specifically point to something masculine that's nearby

steps.m: this (pointing to something in the ayah)
steps.n: Added alif with bar (ٰ) and fatha marks to show proper pronunciation. This word is pointing to something mentioned in the previous part of the verse.

steps.m: from (connects فَضْلِ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or its origin. Here it connects 'favor/grace' (فَضْلِ) to the previous statement, showing this is 'from' that favor.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of giving extra - which becomes favor or grace
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to 'my Lord' mentioned right after it in the ayah

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who takes complete care of others
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: one who takes complete care of others (connected to another word)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it
Adding ى at the end shows this Lord belongs to me or is connected to me
Changed root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding the vowel 'a' between letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لِ to show purpose - 'in order to' or 'so that'
Added نِى to show the object (me) being tested
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense first person (I)
Added questioning hamza at the beginning to make it into a question

steps.n: This is a question particle that connects two alternatives. Here it connects to the previous question ءَأَشْكُرُ (should I be grateful) to ask 'should I be grateful or ungrateful?'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: I reject/am ungrateful (in أَمْ أَكْفُرُ - 'or I am ungrateful')
steps.n: Changed to first person present tense. In this ayah, it's part of a choice between being grateful or ungrateful (أَءَشْكُرُ أَمْ أَكْفُرُ - whether I will be grateful or ungrateful)
This combines two particles: و meaning 'and' and من meaning 'who/whoever'
Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make it past tense. This shows someone (he) did this action in the past.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it
Adding the connective particle فَ to indicate sequence/result

steps.m: so/then surely only (connects to كَفَرَ - whoever disbelieved)
steps.n: Adding مَا limits or restricts the meaning, like saying 'only' or 'just' in English. This word connects to كَفَرَ (whoever disbelieved) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to show ongoing action
The words are actually the same - just written slightly differently. Both mean 'he thanks' in the present tense
Added short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make the basic word for 'soul/self'
Added preposition لِ meaning 'for/to'
Added possessive pronoun هِ meaning 'his/him'
This is a basic particle that means 'whoever' - it's used to talk about any person who might do something
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the ayah

steps.m: he rejected/was ungrateful (subject in وَمَن كَفَرَ)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is part of the phrase 'and whoever rejects/is ungrateful' in the ayah.
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. The shadda (ّ) shows this strong meaning.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what happened before, like saying 'then' or 'so' in English
Doubling the ب adds emphasis to show complete mastery/lordship
The kasra shows this word is connected to something after it or owned by something
Adding ى shows it belongs to me (the speaker). This word comes after مِن فَضْلِ (from the favor of) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Forms an intensive adjective showing complete self-sufficiency. This word describes رَبِّى (my Lord) in this ayah.
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to show this is a strong quality
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing a quality in general terms. In this ayah, it's describing Allah's attribute of being noble and generous.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. This shows the action happened in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to change or alter something from its known state

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and shows doing the action to something else
The وا at the end makes this a command to a group of people
Basic preposition indicating direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added feminine pronoun ها meaning 'her' to show this refers to her throne (عَرْشَهَا) mentioned right after this word in the ayah

steps.m: to raise up, to construct something elevated

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This creates the word for a raised platform or seat of authority.
Added ها at the end to show the throne belongs to her (the Queen of Sheba)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added ن at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed vowels to show present tense
Added sukun at end to make it a command/suggestion form. In this ayah, they are suggesting to test whether the queen will recognize her altered throne.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he guided' in the past tense
Added تَ at start to mean 'she' and changed pattern to show someone finding/seeking guidance for themselves rather than guiding others
Added أَ at beginning to make it a question, and lengthened final ي to show emphasis

steps.m: or (asking which of two options in أَتَهْتَدِىٓ ... أَمْ)
steps.n: This is a question particle that connects two options in a question. Here it connects 'will she be guided' with 'or will she be among those not guided', asking which of these two possibilities will happen.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: she is/exists (subject of نَنظُرْ - we will see)
steps.n: Changed from masculine to feminine by replacing يَ with تَ. This verb shows what 'she' (referring back to the queen) will do, as part of what 'we will see'.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلَّذِينَ (those who). This is like adding glue between words to make them flow better when speaking.
Base relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Added ال (the) at the start to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Added ين at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَهْتَدُونَ (they are guided) that follows it in this ayah, making it mean 'they are not guided'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show doing the action to oneself
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing ending to ي
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a particle that shows something happened right after something else, like saying 'when' in English. It's not derived from a root.

steps.m: Then when (connects to جَآءَتْ - she came)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after something else. This word connects to جَآءَتْ (she came) in the ayah.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he came'
Added alif after ج to make it a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming

steps.m: she came (subject of قِيلَ - it was said)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (she). This word is the subject of قِيلَ (it was said) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added ي between first and second root letters to complete passive form
This is the base demonstrative word meaning 'like this' or 'this way'
Added ها at the beginning as an attention-drawing particle to emphasize what's being pointed to

steps.m: Is it like this? (asking about the throne)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question. In this ayah, she's asking about whether her throne looks like this.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun 'throne'
Added 'u' sound at end to show this is the main subject
Adding feminine singular possessive pronoun كِ meaning 'your'. This word is the subject being asked about in the question 'Is this your throne?'
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
The verb قَوَلَ changes to قَالَ first (flipping و to ا), then adding تْ at the end makes it 'she said' instead of 'he said'. This word shows her response about the throne.
Starting with the basic comparison particle ك with its core meaning
Added أَنَّ which combines with كَ to create the meaning 'as if'

steps.m: as if it (referring to عَرْشُكِ - your throne)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (it) to show what is being compared. Here it refers back to 'your throne' (عَرْشُكِ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a personal pronoun that refers back to 'your throne' (عَرْشُكِ) in this ayah. It's being used to confirm that yes, this is indeed the same throne.
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed from 'to give' to 'was given' by adding dots above (ُ and ِ) to make it passive
Added و between first and second letters because this is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are receiving the action
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific knowledge
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb وَأُوتِينَا (we were given)
This is a preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with قَبْلِهَا (before her/it) to show knowledge was given before something happened.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra at the end because it follows the preposition مِن
Added ها at the end to show this means 'before her'
Changed the root letters into past tense by adding the vowel marks 'a' sounds
Changed from 'he was' to 'they (girls) were' by adding a double n sound at the end
Added 'naa' (نا) at the end to change from 'they were' to 'we were'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb

Regular masculine plural suffix indicating multiple male subjects
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added shadda by combining the doubled middle letter to show intensity
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: and he strongly blocked her (from worshipping Allah)
steps.n: Added ها with fatha to show the action was done to 'her' (referring to the Queen of Sheba in this ayah)

steps.m: what (that which she used to worship)
steps.n: This word connects to تَّعْبُدُ (worship) to ask about what was being worshipped. It's like asking 'What thing did she worship?'
Adding the fatha (َ) shows this is a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern

steps.m: she was (subject of تَّعْبُدُ - she worships)
steps.n: Added ت to show the subject is feminine. This word tells us about what she (the Queen of Sheba) used to do in the past.
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to sukun, and final vowels to damma
Changed from 'he worships' to 'you/she worship' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start
Added shadda on ت because it comes after another ت in كَانَت
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it shows that 'she was from/of (belonged to) a disbelieving people'.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under م to show how to pronounce it correctly. This word connects to قَوْمٍ (people) to show she was from/of the people.

steps.m: to be lower or beneath something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower than'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, showing it means 'besides Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The word for 'deity' became the special name 'Allah' by adding 'ال' (the) and changing some letters. The ِ (kasra) at the end shows it connects to another word after it.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed she (emphasizes that she was from كٰفِرِينَ people)
steps.n: Added ها which refers to the queen of Sheba (Bilqis) who is being discussed in this part of the story. This connects to the next part that explains she was from disbelieving people.
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb
Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (she). This word is the subject of تَّعْبُدُ (she used to worship) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with دُونِ to mean 'from besides/other than'.

Basic past tense verb form showing 'he' did the action of disbelieving. Like changing 'disbelieve' to 'he disbelieved'.
Changes from verb 'he disbelieved' to noun meaning 'disbeliever'
Makes the word plural, meaning 'multiple disbelievers'

steps.m: to her (object of قِيلَ - it was said)

Adding the definite article makes the noun definite and specific

steps.m: then when (connects to رَأَتْهُ - she saw it)

steps.m: she thought/considered (subject of رَأَتْهُ - she saw it)
steps.n: Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is the queen of Sheba, who is mentioned in رَأَتْهُ (she saw it)

steps.m: she thought/considered it (referring to الصَّرْحَ - the palace floor)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what was being considered (the palace floor mentioned as الصَّرْحَ earlier in the ayah)

steps.m: to be deep or vast like an ocean

steps.m: indeed it (referring to صَرْحٌ - palace floor)

Changes from verb root to concrete noun meaning a tall structure

Connected the letters with basic vowels to form past tense verb

Adding مُ at start shows this describes something that was affected by the action
Adding ٌ shows this word is the subject or predicate in the sentence

The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a voweled letter in speech, making the م sound stronger. This word connects to قَوَارِيرَ (crystal clear glass) in this ayah, showing what something is made from.

steps.m: to settle or remain in place

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the word مِن (from/of) in this ayah

steps.m: to say or speak (focusing on meaningful speech)

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Adding ت at the end shows a female (she) did the action. Here it refers to the Queen of Sheba speaking to Solomon.

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely

steps.m: master who cares for others (belonging to العٰلَمِينَ - all worlds)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to what comes after it (العٰلَمِينَ - all worlds)
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'truly' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to رَبِّ - my Lord)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This connects to the next word رَبِّ (my Lord) as I am speaking to my Lord.

steps.m: to do wrong or act unjustly

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the last vowel to a sukun (ْ) to prepare for adding the ending

steps.m: I did wrong (subject speaking to رَبِّ)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action. In this ayah, the queen is speaking to her Lord (رَبِّ)

Added ى with kasra (ِ) at the end to show this means 'my self'. This word is what receives the action in ظَلَمْتُ (I wronged myself).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at the start to make it mean 'to submit oneself' specifically to Allah, rather than just general submission
Added تُ to show the speaker did the action

steps.m: and I submitted (to Allah's will, in response to seeing the truth)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the Queen of Sheba's previous statement about wronging herself, showing this is her response to recognizing the truth
This is a preposition that shows being together with someone/something. Here it connects to سُلَيْمٰنَ (Solomon) to show submission happened 'with Solomon' or 'along with Solomon'.
This name is related to the concept of peace/safety (سلام). While it's a proper name borrowed from Hebrew, understanding its connection to peace helps remember its meaning.
Added vowels and ي to create diminutive form, making it more personal
Added ٰن at the end to complete the name form. The alif with overbar (ٰ) shows lengthening of the sound.
Added fatha at the end because this name is the object of the word مَعَ (with) in the phrase 'with Solomon'
This is the unique name of God in Arabic. The word comes from the root meaning 'divine' but has special spelling and pronunciation rules.
Added لِ at the beginning to show that something belongs to or is for Allah. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it.
This root carries the basic meaning of nurturing and caring for something to help it grow and develop

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter with shadda to show intensity of the nurturing quality

steps.m: to know deeply or mark something to recognize it

Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing middle vowel to sukun makes it mean 'cause to send'

Added the small alif (ٰ) at the end to show the proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the name of an ancient Arab tribe mentioned in the Quran. In this ayah, it's the object of the preposition إِلَىٰ (to), showing who the message was sent to.
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the preposition إِلَىٰ (to)
Connected the root letters and added fatha to make the basic noun form
Changed to show this is the object being sent in the phrase 'We sent their brother'
Adds هُمْ meaning 'their' to show the brother belongs to them (referring to ثَمُودَ / Thamud)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show someone who has this quality

steps.m: a righteous one (object of أَرْسَلْنَآ/we sent)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sent. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this case.
This is a basic particle that connects speech, used here to introduce what was commanded: 'worship Allah'

Changed alif to hamzat wasl (ٱ) and added وا to make it plural command
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being worshipped in اعْبُدُوا)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل and fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being worshipped. The word connects to اعْبُدُوا (worship) in this ayah.

Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before, showing that one thing leads to another
This is a basic pronoun for 'they' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers to the group that is mentioned as disputing in يَخْتَصِمُونَ (they dispute).

steps.m: to split or separate into parts

steps.n: Changed from root letters to a past tense verb meaning 'became separated/afraid' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: two groups (that are disputing with each other in يَخْتَصِمُونَ)
steps.n: Added انِ to show there are exactly two groups. These groups are the subject of يَخْتَصِمُونَ (they dispute with each other)

steps.m: to argue or dispute with someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action together
Added fathas to make the basic past tense form. This shows the action happened in the past.

Becomes a noun meaning a group of people who stand together as one. The root meaning of standing/rising is still connected to how people stand together as a community.

steps.m: O my people! (being called by صالح)
steps.n: Two changes happen: 1) يٰ is added at the start to call out to someone (like saying 'Hey!' or 'O!'), and 2) ِ is added at the end to show these are 'my' people. This is Salih calling out to his people in the ayah.
This is the basic preposition that means 'for' or 'to'

steps.m: the desperately needy one (who يُجِيبُ responds to)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and a fatha at the end because it's the object of the verb يُجِيبُ (responds to)
This is a particle that introduces a time condition. In this ayah, it connects 'when' to دَعَاهُ (he calls upon Him), showing when something happens.

steps.m: to call out to, to summon

steps.n: Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.

steps.m: he called out to him (object of يُجِيبُ - responds to)
steps.n: Added هُ (him) to show who is being called out to. This word shows who يُجِيبُ (responds to) is acting upon - the one being called.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to ِ, and adding ُ at end
Adds وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous verb يُجِيبُ (answers). This verb acts on السُّوٓءَ (hardship) which comes after it in the ayah.
Changed the action into a thing by adding ُ on the first letter. Like changing 'to harm' into 'harm'

steps.m: the harm (that is being removed in يَكْشِفُ)
steps.n: Added ٱل to show we're talking about a specific harm. Also doubled the س with ّ because it comes after ٱل. The long و is shown with ٓ.
Added َ at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يَكْشِفُ (removes)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb
Changes past tense to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The ُ at the end shows it's happening now.
Adds كُمْ to show who is being made into something (you all). This connects to خُلَفَآءَ (successors) later in the ayah which explains what they are being made into.

steps.m: and he makes/sets you all (as successors)
steps.n: Adds وَ (and) to connect this with the previous phrase about answering the distressed one and removing evil

steps.m: to come after or replace something

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ي and ة to make it mean 'one who succeeds/replaces'
Changed from one successor to many successors by adding اء at the end and changing the middle vowels
Added َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَجْعَلُكُمْ (makes you)
Added marks (fatha and sukun) to make it mean a specific earth/land rather than just the general concept

steps.m: the earth (object being acted upon in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it's being acted upon in the sentence
Root letters أله became a noun إِلَه meaning 'a god' - something that is worshipped
The small alif (ٰ) makes the 'aa' sound longer when reading

steps.m: is there a god? (questioning the existence of another deity besides اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added أَء at the start to turn it into a question ('Is there...?'), and added ٌ at the end to show we're asking about any god in general
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows when things happen or exist together.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a pause in speech, making the م sound stronger. It connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to mean 'with Allah'.
Changed the first letter and added shadda to make it specifically refer to Allah, the One True God

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to what comes before)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word

steps.m: to be small in amount or number

steps.n: Adding vowels to show this describes a quality of being small/few
Added ي to make it an adjective describing how much of something there is

steps.m: a little (describing how much is remembered in مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how much of something. In this ayah, it describes how little people remember/take heed.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'what'
The shadda (ّ) is added to emphasize the word. This makes the 'm' sound stronger.
Adding a shadda (double mark) on the middle letter makes the meaning more intense or active
Adding تَ at the start shows doing the action to yourself
Adding ون shows that many people are doing the action together
Starting with the two separate words that combine to form this compound: أَم (or) and مَن (who)

steps.m: Who is it that... (introducing a rhetorical question)
steps.n: The two words combine with a shadda, creating a special questioning word that introduces rhetorical questions to emphasize Allah's power
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last vowel changes to kasra (ِ).
Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is being done to multiple people being addressed
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects 'guides you' with 'darknesses', showing where the guiding happens.
Added the kasra vowel mark under ف and changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance of darkness
Changed to plural form by adding ات and changing middle vowel to ُ
Changed ending to -i sound because it comes after the word 'in/through' (في) which always adds this sound to the next word
Doubling the ر makes the meaning stronger
Adding ال makes it specific/definite

steps.m: of the land (contrasted with وَٱلْبَحْرِ - and the sea)
steps.n: Added kasra (i-sound) to show this word means 'of the land' - paired with 'of the sea' later in the verse

The noun form comes from the idea of water splitting or cutting through the land. In this ayah, the word is in the genitive case because it is possessed by 'darknesses' (ظُلُمٰتِ), forming the phrase 'darknesses of the sea'.
Adding ال makes it 'the sea' and ِ shows it follows a preposition
Adding وَ connects this word to البَرِّ (the land) mentioned before it, forming the phrase 'the land and the sea'
This is a question word asking about who does something. Here it's asking who sends the winds.
Added و at the beginning to connect this question with the previous one in the ayah, asking about multiple aspects of Allah's power
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The word changed from past tense 'sent' to present tense 'sends' by adding يُ at the start. The ُ ending connects it to 'whoever' as the doer of the action.
Root letters changed to make the noun 'wind' - the middle letter و became ي with a kasra sound
Added ا after ي to make it plural (more than one wind)
Added ال at the beginning to mean 'the' and doubled the ر with shadda because it comes after ال
Changed 'al' to a shorter form, stretched the middle 'a' sound, and added a mark at the end because it's receiving the action of sending

steps.m: to announce happiness or good news

steps.n: Changed from a verb meaning 'to give good news' into a noun meaning 'good news' itself

steps.m: as good news (describing how the winds are sent in يُرْسِلُ ٱلرِّيٰحَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this describes how the winds are sent - like saying 'He sends the winds as good news'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to create a preposition. This word comes before رَحْمَتِهِ (His mercy) in the ayah.
Root letters joined together to make the word for 'hand'
Added alif maqsura at the end to make it dual form. In this ayah, it's used figuratively in the phrase 'between the hands of His mercy' to mean 'before/in front of His mercy'
Adding sukun to mark the end of the word. This is a common way to end Arabic words.

steps.m: to show mercy, be merciful, have compassion

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to noun meaning 'mercy' by adding vowels

steps.m: a specific instance or manifestation of mercy
steps.n: The taa marbouta makes this a noun indicating one instance of mercy
The word 'mercy' gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's connected to another word that owns or possesses it
Adding هِ shows this mercy belongs to Allah (like saying 'His mercy')
Root letters أ-ل-ه become the noun إِلَاه meaning 'a god' - like changing 'worship' into 'the one who is worshipped'
The long 'aa' sound is written with a small alif above instead of a full alif
Added tanwin dammah to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' instead of 'the')
Added أَ and hamza to make this a question. This word is being compared to Allah in this ayah, asking if there is any other deity with Allah.
This is a basic preposition that shows accompaniment or being together with something. It's one of the fundamental particles in Arabic that isn't derived from a root.
The shadda emphasizes the م because it comes after a question word. This word is part of the phrase أَءِلٰهٌ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ meaning 'is there any god with Allah?'
Changed to the unique form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word itself

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by رَحْمَتِهِ - His mercy)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added kasra at end to show possession. This word is possessed by رَحْمَتِهِ (His mercy) in this ayah.
This root carries the basic meaning of being high or elevated, both physically and in status
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The و at the end changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.
Added تَ at beginning and changed the middle vowel to show this is a special form indicating supreme elevation. This form is often used for Allah to show His absolute supremacy.
Changed the root into a noun form that means 'one who is worshipped'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name for The One God by adding ال. The two ل letters combine with a shadda to show extra importance.
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in تَعٰلَى (He is exalted). The ٱ is just a different way to write the initial alif.
Starting with the two separate words that will combine. عَنْ is a preposition meaning 'away from' or 'about', and مَا means 'what'
When عَنْ combines with مَا, the ن assimilates into م with a shadda, and they become one word
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
The ون ending shows that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action together. In this ayah, it refers to those who worship others alongside Allah.
Starting with the two separate question words: أَم (or) and مَن (who)

steps.n: The two words merge together with a shadda, making one word that asks 'or who?' This word is asking about who begins creation, referring to the next word يَبْدَؤُا (begins)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowel marks
Changed the ending to show many people (they) instead of one person (he)

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something from nothing

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels creates the past tense active verb pattern
Changes from verb 'created' to noun 'creation' by modifying middle vowel to sukun and keeping fatha on first letter. This word is what is being begun/initiated in the phrase 'Who begins creation'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific creation rather than creation in general. This word is what is being begun/initiated in the phrase 'Who begins the creation'
This is a connecting word that shows the next step in a sequence. Here it connects the first creation (يَبْدَؤُا۟) with its repetition (يُعِيدُهُ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (happening now), and changed middle vowels to show someone is making something/someone else return

steps.m: he returns/brings it back (object of يَبْدَؤُا)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what is being returned. This word is connected to يَبْدَؤُا (he begins) in the ayah, showing what is being returned.
This is a combination of two particles: و (and) + من (who/whoever)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added كُم to show who receives the provision - in this ayah, Allah is providing sustenance to all people
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The fatha is added at the end because it connects to the next word.
Added shadda for emphasis and smoother pronunciation when connecting to the next word.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated
steps.n: Basic meaning of the root
Adds اء ending to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: of/from the sky/heaven (source of رِزْق provision)
steps.n: Changed to show 'from the sky' by adding kasra at the end, with special writing style using ٱل and longer alif
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it a noun
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific earth rather than any earth
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it (the sky)
Adding ِ shows this word is connected to مِنَ (from) in the previous part of the verse
Basic meaning of root
Changes from verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
The small alif (ٰ) makes the 'aa' sound longer
Adds tanween to show indefiniteness (like 'a' or 'an' in English)

steps.m: Is there a deity? (questioning the existence of another deity besides اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds أَ at the start to make it a question, showing strong doubt about other gods existing
This is the basic form of the particle meaning 'with' or 'along with'
Added emphasis through shadda (doubling mark) on the first letter. This connects it strongly with the word after it (Allah) to mean 'with Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection. This word is connected to مَّعَ (with) in the phrase 'with Allah'.

Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb

Added plural command marker وا to make it a command for multiple people
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added alif to strengthen the meaning, making it specifically mean a clear or strong proof
Changed the alif to an alif maksura (ٰ) for proper spelling
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the command 'bring forth' (هاتوا)
Added suffix كُمْ to show the proof belongs to the people being addressed
This is a conditional particle that connects two ideas. Here it introduces the condition 'if you are truthful.' The word إِن shows that what follows is a condition that must be met.
The و is a weak letter that affects how the word is formed

steps.m: one who tells the truth/honest person
steps.n: Added ا after first letter to show who does the action, like changing 'speak truth' to 'truth speaker'

steps.m: truthful people (in كُنتُمْ صٰدِقِينَ - you were truthful)

The َ at the end shows this word is in a special grammar position (like an object)
Changed the root into a command by adding a 'oo' sound (damma) and making the middle letter silent (sukun)
The middle letter 'و' is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
Basic negative particle used to negate what comes after it
Added shadda to emphasize the negation. This stronger 'not' connects to the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows) to strongly deny that anyone knows what's in the heavens and earth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb

Question word used to ask about people. In this ayah, it's asking 'who knows?' where مَن is the subject of the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows).
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it connects 'knows' (يَعْلَمُ) with 'the heavens' (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ), showing where the knowledge exists.
Changed final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word from an action to a place/thing by adding ء at the end
Made the word plural by adding ات at the end and changing ء to و
Added ٱل at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the س (shown by ّ)
Added ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in). The alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for earth/ground
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word السماوات (the heavens)

steps.m: to be hidden or absent from sight/knowledge
unseen realm/matter (what is being known in يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'the unseen'. This word is what is being known about in relation to يَعْلَمُ (knows) in this ayah.

steps.m: the unseen realm (what is being known in يَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and َ to show it's being acted upon by the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows)
This is an exception particle that shows nothing/nobody knows except Allah. It connects the previous statement 'nobody knows' to the next word 'Allah', showing He is the only one who knows.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this word is the subject
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word negates يَشْعُرُونَ (they perceive/know) in the ayah.

steps.m: to perceive or be aware of something

steps.n: The vowel pattern shows this verb is about acquiring a quality (awareness)
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and adjusting vowels

steps.m: they perceive (referring to those in the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added plural marker ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers back to those mentioned in the heavens and earth (مَن فِى ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ) in the ayah.

steps.m: when (asking about يُبْعَثُونَ - they will be resurrected)
steps.n: This is a question word used specifically to ask about time, especially for important future events. In this ayah, it's asking about when the resurrection will happen. It's connected to the verb يُبْعَثُونَ (they will be resurrected) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed vowels to show passive voice - something being done to someone rather than someone doing it
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show present/future tense passive
Added ون at the end to show plural (many people being raised)
This is a basic particle used to change or correct what was said before
Added kasra at the end because it connects to the next word ٱدَّارَكَ (attained). This is just a connection helper and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together

steps.m: he tried to catch up with (someone)
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show effort or attempt to do something with someone else
Added ت at beginning to show people doing something together or one after another

steps.m: they caught up with each other
steps.n: Added ا at start to show Form VIII pattern, د merges with ت to become دّ
Changed the root into a noun by adding the kasra (ِ) under the ع and sukun (ْ) on the ل. This transforms the idea of knowing into the concrete thing 'knowledge'
Added ُهُمْ at the end to show this knowledge belongs to them. The ُ (damma) connects هُمْ to the word.
Basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it shows that the disbelievers are 'in' doubt concerning the Hereafter.
The ي changes to ى when written at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed from a basic root meaning 'late' to an active participle meaning 'coming later/last' by adding alif after first letter
Added ة to change from 'later/last' to mean specifically 'the afterlife' - like how we say 'the hereafter'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in/concerning)

steps.m: but rather (contradicting what was said about هُمْ)
steps.n: This word shows that what follows (they are in doubt) contradicts what was assumed before. It's like saying 'Actually, the truth is...'
This is a pronoun that refers to a group of people (all male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it's the subject doing the action عَمُونَ (they are blind).
This is a preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'their knowledge' (عِلْمُهُمْ) with 'the Hereafter' (ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ) to show where their knowledge is lacking.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word in the Uthmani script of the Quran
The middle letter is doubled to show strong/intense doubt
Changed to show 'in doubt' by adding two dots under (tanween) because it follows the word 'in' (فِى)
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. In this ayah, it connects to the word عَمُونَ (blind) to show what they are blind about.

steps.m: from it (referring to الْءَاخِرَةِ - the Hereafter)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to 'the Hereafter' mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows what they are blind about.
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a sukun (هُم), making the pronunciation clearer

steps.m: rather/but (correcting what was said about عِلْمُهُمْ)
steps.n: This is a particle that corrects or contradicts what was said before. Here it's showing that rather than having knowledge, they are in doubt.
This is a standalone pronoun that refers to three or more males. In this ayah, it's the subject (doer) of the verb عَمُونَ (they are blind).
This is a basic preposition showing the starting point or source of something
Added shadda on م due to assimilation with previous word

steps.m: from it/her (referring to الْآخِرَةِ - the Hereafter)
steps.n: Added هَا which refers back to الْآخِرَةِ (the Hereafter) mentioned earlier in the verse

Added ن to complete the present tense plural form. This word describes the state of هُم (they) in the ayah.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb

This is the basic form of the word used to connect descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who' in English

Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. The و is pronounced with a 'u' sound (ُ).

Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The middle letter و drops out because it's a weak letter. The vowels change to match the pattern.
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. The ّ (shadda) is added to strengthen the sound.

steps.m: dust, soil (object of كُنَّا - we were)
steps.n: The word gets an -an ending to show it's receiving the action 'we were'. The alif is written specially with a small line on top.

steps.m: to nurture/care for (as a father does)

Added conjunction و (and). Added proper spelling with hamza and long alif
Starting with the basic emphatic particle that shows certainty
Added ا at the end to show this is about 'we/us'

steps.m: one who is made to come out

steps.m: surely ones who are brought out

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment to someone

steps.m: we were promised (subject of this statement)

This is the basic attention-getting part that points to something
